title: Filter Bar — Search, Tag, Status, Domain, Sort, and URL Sync
problem: The feature tree renders all 27 features at once with no way to search or narrow down — visitors looking for a specific tag, domain, or status must scroll the whole tree manually. Filter state is also not shareable via URL.

lineage.spawnReason: scope split: add a FilterBar component above the tree/graph views that supports full-text search, tag/status/domain chip filters, priority sort, graph colorBy toggle, and expand/collapse all — all URL-synced via replaceState

analysis: The FilterBar sits above the feature tree/graph and co-ordinates four independent filter axes: full-text search (featureKey, title, problem, analysis, implementation, successCriteria, tags, decisions), tag chips, status chips, and domain chips. Each axis is stored as URL-synced state (URLSearchParams via history.replaceState) so any filter combination is shareable and survives refresh. A collapsible panel keeps the UI compact — only the search input and a 'Filters N' button are always visible; chip rows appear on demand. When a filter is active, ancestor nodes of matching features are automatically added to treeFilterKeys so the tree retains lineage context instead of orphaning matches. Matching ancestors are also auto-expanded so results are immediately visible without manual unfolding. A sort control (tree order vs priority) and a graph colorBy control (status vs domain) live in the same panel to unify all view-control state in one place. Expand-all / collapse-all buttons appear in the tree toolbar. A match pill (e.g. '12 / 27') shows filtered vs total counts.
implementation: FilterBar is a controlled React component receiving all filter state and callbacks from App. App manages: searchQuery (string), activeTags (Set<string>), activeStatuses (Set<string>), activeDomains (Set<string>), sortBy ('default'|'priority'), graphColorBy ('status'|'domain') — all initialised from URLSearchParams on mount and synced back in a single consolidated useEffect. filteredFeatures is derived via useMemo: for each feature, search query is matched against a concatenated hay string; tag/status/domain sets are ANDed. treeFilterKeys (Set<string>) extends filteredFeatures with all their ancestors so tree nodes that provide lineage context remain visible. A separate useEffect auto-deletes matched keys from collapsedKeys when a filter activates. allTags is derived by counting tag occurrences across all features and sorting by frequency descending (most-used first). allDomains is derived as a deduplicated, alphabetically-sorted list. FilterBar renders: a search <input type='search'>, an expand/open button with active-count badge, and a collapsible panel with Status / Domain / Tags / Sort / Color-by sections. Tag chips show count badges when count > 1; domain chips show count badges always. A '✕ Clear filters' button appears when any filter is active — it resets searchQuery, activeTags, activeStatuses, and activeDomains but does NOT reset sortBy or graphColorBy (those are view preferences, not filters). Expand-all / collapse-all buttons live in the view-toolbar (outside FilterBar) and are only shown in tree view. FeatureGraph receives filteredFeatures so graph view honours the same filter state.

decisions.decision: All filter axes stored in URL via history.replaceState
decisions.rationale: Filter state is a meaningful reading position — sharing a URL with active filters lets collaborators see the same focused view without re-applying filters manually.
alternativesConsidered: Component state only — not shareable, resets on navigation
localStorage — persists but not shareable across devices

decisions.decision: Collapsible panel: always-visible search + badge button, chips on demand
decisions.rationale: Showing all chip rows permanently would consume 200px+ of vertical space above the tree. A single expand button with an active-count badge keeps the UI compact while signalling when filters are in effect.
alternativesConsidered: Always-visible chips — takes too much vertical space
Separate filter page/modal — breaks spatial context with the tree

decisions.decision: Ancestor nodes of matches included in treeFilterKeys
decisions.rationale: Filtering to only exact matches would orphan child features from their parents, breaking the lineage narrative. Including ancestors preserves tree shape while focusing the visible set.
alternativesConsidered: Show only exact matches — hides lineage context
Show matches + full subtrees — too permissive, defeats the filter

decisions.decision: Sort and graph colorBy live in FilterBar, not separate controls
decisions.rationale: These are view-control options that belong alongside filter options — they affect what the user sees and how, which is the same semantic category as filters.
alternativesConsidered: Separate sort button next to tree/graph toggle — scatters related controls
Always sort by priority — removes user agency

successCriteria: Typing in the search box filters the tree in real-time; matching features and their ancestors remain visible while non-matching branches disappear. Tag, status, and domain chips toggle filter axes independently. Sort 'priority' reorders the tree by P1–P5; 'tree order' restores insertion order. Graph colorBy switches node colours between status palette and domain-hue palette. filter/sort/colorBy state is reflected in the URL and survives page reload. A match pill shows 'N / 27' when any filter is active. '✕ Clear filters' resets search and chip filters but preserves sort and colorBy preferences.
knownLimitations: Filter is applied to the flat allFeatures list — a feature deeply nested under a non-matching ancestor is still shown if it matches, which can produce out-of-context results in priority-sort mode
No debounce on search input — re-filters on every keystroke (acceptable at 29 features, may need debounce at scale)
Expand-all / collapse-all are not persisted in URL — collapse state resets on reload
colorBy and sortBy are only visible in FilterBar panel when the relevant view is active — easy to miss when switching views
clearFilters() does not reset sortBy or graphColorBy — users must manually change these in the panel

revisions.author: claude
revisions.reason: Initial field population — FilterBar fully implemented in App.tsx; all fields authored from code inspection

revisions.author: claude
revisions.reason: Fixed two inaccuracies: clearFilters() does not reset sortBy/graphColorBy (they persist as view preferences); allTags sorted by frequency descending and allDomains sorted alphabetically — both now documented in implementation. Updated successCriteria to match actual clear behavior. Updated feature count in KL from 27 to 29.

statusHistory.reason: Two inaccuracies: clearFilters() does not reset sortBy or graphColorBy (those persist across clear); tag list is sorted by frequency descending, domain list alphabetically — both omitted from implementation.

annotations.author: lac advance
annotations.body: Two inaccuracies: clearFilters() does not reset sortBy or graphColorBy (those persist across clear); tag list is sorted by frequency descending, domain list alphabetically — both omitted from implementation.
